programmes.show_description: Jack finds a bag stuffed full of money and has grand plans for a life of luxury, but they soon fall flat. S2 E3: The Gravy Train
It's 1962 and Gary Sparrow has been trapped in the past for 17 years, but his decision to witness his own birth sees him catapulted into the 21st century: Many Happy Returns (2016 Special)
Rowan Atkinson stars as the hapless Mr. Bean. In this original award-winning episode, he takes an exam, sunbathes and goes to church with Richard Briers. Ep1: Mr. Bean
As CID investigates a murder linked to a crime syndicate, Sam thinks he is finally coming to grips with the world of 1973, until he meets his 29-year-old father. Starring John Simm. S1 E8
The holiday fund is full of IOUs, the housekeeping fund is overspent, and Sandra and Beryl need money fast and so a trip to the auction room beckons. S2 E3: Housekeeping
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Pamela and Brenda are not on good terms as Pamela has ordered Brenda out; in a attempt to reform her character, Brenda agrees to accompany Malcolm on his day off at a motor museum. S1 E5: Leaving
Tracey gets a letter from her auntie Vera who is retiring as a Pearly Queen and wants her to take over, but Tracey faces competition from a jealous Sharon. S11 E4: The Girls With The Pearl Buttons
Mr. Bean wreaks havoc at Harrods when he goes Christmas shopping, then has trouble stuffing a turkey on Christmas Day. E7: Merry Christmas, Mr. Bean
With Amy having sent all his clothes to the cleaners, David looks doomed to spend the rest of his holiday in drag and gets no sympathy from his wife or the Cochrans. S2 E1: Manana

Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S4, E2: A Young Man's Fancy
David goes to a casino where he meets the Cochrans and hopes that Linda will bring him luck as he puts his winning system into play. S2 E2: Casino
Lord Meldrum decrees that his brother - The Honourable Teddy - will marry Madge or go to work in the family business to keep his mind off servant girls. S2 E1 Labour or Love
Mr Spooner annoys the household by playing his radio loudly - Rigsby is annoyed, but he finds it difficult to pluck up the courage to confront him about it. S1 E5: All Our Yesterdays
After surprising him by paying back a debt in full and on time, Harry Fenning invites Wolfie to help him out with a bit of work... but it'll involve sourcing his own car. S3 E3: The Big Job
Aveline has an unfortunate experience on the catwalk, Freddie and Lilo Lill have a shouting match, and Joey is blackmailed. S3 E6

Sandra is devastated at splitting up with Peter Crawford, so Beryl decides to find him and bring him back to their flat - is it a case of mistaken identity when he arrives? S2 E2: Look Before You Leap
British Railways issues Mr Parkin with a van for his job, but he needs to learn how to drive it. S2 E4: The Van
Lord Meldrum decrees that his brother - The Honourable Teddy - will marry Madge or go to work in the family business to keep his mind off servant girls. S2 E1 Labour or Love
George needs help finding the answers for all the competitions he enters - luckily, Robin is starting a new job selling encyclopaedias and is looking for his first buyer. S5 E3: A Little Knowledge
The school hosts a charity bazaar and needs a celebrity to be the Master of Ceremonies. Max claims to be friends with a British comedian, but turns out he's never met him. S3 E7: The School Fete
Alf Garnett is spurred into action by the arrival of his AIDS leaflet. S3 E2

Since he can't sell his second hand cars, Arthur decides it might be a good idea to hire them out instead - but Ray has some trouble with the customers. S9 E10: Cars and Pints and Pains
British Railways issues Mr Parkin with a van for his job, but he needs to learn how to drive it. S2 E4: The Van
Gary Sparrow is an unhappily married TV repairman who one day walks into a time warp. Nicholas Lyndhurst stars in the hit 1990s sitcom. S1 E1: Rites of Passage
Mr. Bean turns the most ordinary situations into moments of excruciating embarrassment as he visits a department store and gets carried away. E2: The Return of Mr. Bean
Sam encounters young Tony Crane, a villain he put away in 2006. He realises he is faced with the chance every copper dreams about - stopping the killer before he kills. Starring John Simm. S2 E1
Sandra needs the flat to entertain her new boyfriend Danny, meaning that Beryl pretends to be with her boyfriend but is visiting Gerry in the flat downstairs instead. S2 E4: The Proposal
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
With a head full of compassion, Brenda has ideas about making Pamela feel more appreciated by her lover Sidney Clough. S1 E6: Repenting
Dorien's book group is convening to discuss Crime and Punishment, which turns out to be rather applicable to the events that unfold in Tracey's household. S11 E5: Tracey's Choice
More hilarious mishaps with the bumbling Mr. Bean, this time as he books into a hotel room and makes himself well and truly at home but locks himself out. E8: Mr. Bean in Room 426
David goes to a casino where he meets the Cochrans and hopes that Linda will bring him luck as he puts his winning system into play. S2 E2: Casino

Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S4, E3: When You've Got To Go
When a young English couple arrive at the hotel, Amy and Linda assume that they are newlyweds because they are so evidently in love. S2 E3: Couples
Teddy causes a mass walk-out at the factory, the Prime Minister is coming to dinner, and Alf is consumed by thoughts of Lady Lavender - or rather her money. S2 E2: Trouble at the Mill
When Miss Jones sees a prowler through her window, Rigsby suspects everyone else as a police detective appears and begins to investigate. S1 E6: The Prowler
Tofkin is back sniffing around the pub and appears to have Wolfie and the boys by the short 'n' curlies, but they're not who his eyes are set on. S3 E4: Tofkin's Revenge
Freddie is arrested for stealing a dustcart, Grandad is taken to hospital, Billy quarrels with Julie and Jack is nearly seduced by Carmen. S3 E7
The holiday fund is full of IOUs, the housekeeping fund is overspent, and Sandra and Beryl need money fast and so a trip to the auction room beckons. S2 E3: Housekeeping
There is talk of a wildcat strike among drivers - if any Hatley staff join it, the station could be closed. S2 E5: Lucky Strike
Teddy causes a mass walk-out at the factory, the Prime Minister is coming to dinner, and Alf is consumed by thoughts of Lady Lavender - or rather her money. S2 E2: Trouble at the Mill
While the flatmates compete to get the flat to themselves to entertain friends, George is trying to find jobs to avoid Mildred. S5 E4: Love and Let Love
Jeremy Brown's responsibility as an English teacher to foreign students doesn't always end when he leaves the classroom. S3 E8: What a Tangled Web
When Alf Garnett is given a cordless telephone it turns out to be more of a hindrance than a help. S3 E3
